mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of PAYEMS growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates monetary easing that lowers discount rates for equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening demand for construction, which correlates with lower corporate revenues and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in periods of HOUST decline are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signal tightening monetary policy that raises borrowing costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of co-increases in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect size < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates weakening supply-side capacity, which reduces corporate profits and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in periods of INDPRO decline are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects expanding demand, which raises corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in periods of GDPC1 growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of UNRATE increases are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in real sales (RSAFS) indicates weakening consumer demand, which correlates with lower corporate revenues and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in periods of RSAFS decline are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
